LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GRAYBAR ELECTRIC COMPANY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE NOT DEVOID OF INFORMATION LINKING PLAINTIFF TO EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS FOR WHICH DEFENDANT IS RESPONSIBLE. IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THROUGH THE ASPERIN DECLARATION. THE OBJECTION TO THE ASPERIN DECLARATION WAS OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER CONFORMING TO THE RULING. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-27-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

ASBESTOS MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2007 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Shannon S. Patel of Levin Simes Kaiser & Gornick, 273-8137, appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Cesar A. Alvarado of Archer Norris, 925-930-6600, appearing for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT GRAYBAR ELECTRIC COMPANY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden because plaintiff's interrogatory responses are not devoid of information linking plaintiff to exposure to asbestos for which defendant is responsible. In any event, plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact through the Asperin declaration. The objection to the Asperin declaration was overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order conforming to the ruling. (301/PJB/PM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
